label: nondecomp
classification_reason: The witness is not a harmless boundary of the observable problem. It has full observed overlap, all displayed outcome means bounded away from 0 and 1, \(q_{\cdot\mid1}\neq q_{\cdot\mid0}\), and \(\kappa(P)=3/10\neq0\), which is exactly the corrected active-mediator regime where the strict non-decomposition claim was meant to hold. For every \(0\le\lambda\le3/10\), the sharp shared-\(U\) interval equals the per-mediator box, \(I(\lambda)=B_{\mathrm{box}}(\lambda)=[-\lambda,\lambda]\), so the kernel strictness mechanism is false on the motivating regime. This is not repaired by the natural positivity/overlap convention: the same observed law also has an overlapping two-strata exclusion-corner completion with \(w_0=(1/4,3/4)\), \(w_1=(3/4,1/4)\), \(\pi=(1/2,1/2)\), and structural means \((7/10,3/10)\) for \(m=0\) and \((9/10,1/10)\) for \(m=1\); for \(|t|\le1/15\), setting \(s_t(a)=s_0(a)-3t/2\), \(s_t(b)=s_0(b)+t/2\), \(\varphi(m,0,u)=s_t(u)\), and \(\varphi(m,1,u)=s_t(u)+t\) preserves compatibility, keeps all means in \([0,1]\), and attains \(\theta=t\). Thus disjoint support is only one presentation of a deeper leak-box saturation mechanism. The confirmed sibling "vertex" anchors the endpoint structure as a finite response-type LP, but it does not supply a strictness target that can be recovered from this equality.
witness_property: Common-leak saturation of the leak-average identity. The displayed refutation realizes it through a two-strata disjoint-support completion \(w_0(a)=1\), \(w_1(b)=1\), \(\pi(a)=\pi(b)=1/2\), where unobserved counterfactual cells can receive the same leak increment \(t\). The compatibility equations are unchanged because arm 0 observes only stratum \(a\) and arm 1 observes only stratum \(b\), while the leak-average identity bounds both \(I(\lambda)\) and \(B_{\mathrm{box}}(\lambda)\) by \([-\lambda,\lambda]\) and the construction attains every \(t\in[-\lambda,\lambda]\). The saturation is not merely the zero-overlap corner: an overlapping completion with treatment propensities \(e(a)=3/4\), \(e(b)=1/4\) attains the same equality for small positive \(\lambda\).
lineage_note: The original claim that \([L(\lambda),U(\lambda)]\subsetneq B_{\mathrm{box}}(\lambda)\) and \(\subsetneq B_{\mathrm{sens}}(\lambda)\) for all small positive \(\lambda\) on \(\{P:\kappa(P)\neq0, q_{\cdot\mid1}\neq q_{\cdot\mid0}\}\) was refuted by the rational active-mediator witness with \(I(\lambda)=B_{\mathrm{box}}(\lambda)=[-\lambda,\lambda]\). No mended positive claim is recorded: the standard front-door convention checked in Pearl1995CausalDiagrams (DOI https://doi.org/10.1093/biomet/82.4.669) is an observed graphical/support convention, not a latent anti-saturation rule; adding such a rule would be witness-tailored, while weakening to mere non-strict inclusion would gut the strict non-decomposition question.
obstruction_if_unsalvageable: leak-box saturation inside the intended active-mediator regime: a feasible shared-\(U\) completion can attain every value in the same leak box as the per-mediator relaxation for a whole interval of positive \(\lambda\), even with a standard overlapping latent treatment law, so strict non-decomposition is false rather than missing only a regularity convention.
